generalities.text: Main THE ENCYCLOPEDIA OF PURE MATERIA MEDICA By TIMOTHY F. ALLEN, A.M., M.D. Presented by Médi-T ASARUM. A. Europæum, Linn. Nat. order , Aristolochiaceæ. Common name , Haselwurz. Preparation , Tincture of the root and whole plant. Authorities. [No. 6 , no mention of A. is found in this place; 7 , is a general statement; 8 , case of poisoning (given by Ray); 9 , general statement; 10 , general statement that A. is abortifacient.] 1 , Hahnemann, M. M. Pura, 3, 225; 2 , Stapf, ibid.; 3 , Franz, ibid.; 4 , Rückert, ibid.; 5 , Hornburg, ibid.; 6 , Murray (Appar. Med., 3, 519), ibid.; 7 , Helmont (Pharmac. Mod.), ibid.; 8 , Wedel (Amnit. Mat. Med., p. 240), ibid.; 9 , Cost and Villemet (Essais sur plant. indig., 1778), ibid.; 10 , Ray (Hist. univers. plant.), ibid.; 11 , Linnæus (Flora Suecica). MIND. ► He imagines he is hovering in the air, when walking, like a spirit, [3] . ► Great cheerfulness (after six and twelve hours), sometimes interrupted for a few moments by a melancholy and gloomy mood, [4] . ► Weeping, sadness, and anxious feeling, [2] . ► Melancholy, fretful, [1] . ► Angry and cross, before cough, [1] . ► Feels quite stupid in the head; is not disposed to do anything, [4] . ► As often as he attempts to reflect a little the pains in the head and the nausea increase; he has to abandon his ideas, which cannot be of much use, since he is quite stupid, [4] . ► *Condition of mind as if just falling asleep; gradual vanishing of ideas , [3] . ► His thoughts are so overstrained that they vanish entirely, [3] . [10.] ► *When attempting to perform some intellectual labor, and reflect, his thoughts immediately vanish; there is a drawing pressure in the forehead, and he must at once cease thinking, [4] . ► *Inability to do any kind of work whatever; does not succeed in anything; the mental faculties fail (previous to every vomiting, after which feels somewhat relieved); in general, the mental faculties are deficient during the whole proving, [4] . HEAD. ► Confusion; stupid condition of the whole head, with tension in the region of the ears, [4] . ► Painful tensive confusion of the head, [2] . ► Confusion of the head , less perceptible when walking than when sitting, with pressure in the eyes, as with a dull point from within outwards, especially below the right eyelid (after a quarter of an hour), [3] . ► Sensation of vertigo, as though not standing securely (evening, after four days), [4] . ► Vertigo, as from being slightly intoxicated, when rising from a seat, and walking about (in ten minutes), [2] . ► Early in the morning, when rising, dizziness in the head, with headache in the left forehead (after twenty-two hours), [2] . ► Weight and confusion of the head, with pressure on the sagittal suture, as if he were intoxicated (after three hours), [2] . ► Weight in the head, as if some wabbling body were in it, which presses in the direction the head is turned, forwards or backwards, [3] . [20.] ► Drawing headache, as if it would extend into the temples (at noon); the pain seems to decrease in the open air, and when lying down, [2] . ► (Stupefying) drawing, here and there, in the brain, in the ear, and nape of the neck, [2] . ► Pressure in the brain, mostly forwards (after three-quarters of an hour), [4] . ► Pressure over the greater portion of the brain, from without inwards (after two and three-quarter hours), [4] . ► Pressure in different places in the brain, mingled with various sensations, [4] . ► Dull headache (after half an hour), [5] . ► *Sensation of a more or less violent pressure in the forehead, from above downwards, [4] . ► Pressure in the brain, at a place in the forepart of the head, from above downwards, as if from a stone (after a quarter of an hour), [4] . ► Violent pressure in the forehead, pressing downward upon the eyes, which then water (after two and a half hours), [4] . ► Violent drawing pressure in the brain below the forehead (increased when retching), [4] . [30.] ► Pressive, dull pain in the forehead, causing a dreary feeling, as after being awaked too early, [2] . ► Sharp, pressive headache above the root of the nose, [3] . ► Tearing headache in the forehead for some seconds, after stooping and raising the head again, [4] . ► Tearing, pulsative pain in the forehead, [4] . ► Throbbing pain in the forehead, early in the morning when rising (after twenty-four hours), [4] . ► Stooping excites a throbbing pain in the forehead, [4] . ► Headache and confusion in the left temple, afterwards below the parietal bones, and lastly in the occiput, [5] . ► Pressive pain in the temples, especially the left, [3] . ► *Very sensitive compressive headache in the left temple and behind the ears , more violent when walking or shaking the head, less when sitting (after twelve hours), [2] . ► *Tearing, pressive pain in the left temple, [2] . [40.] ► Itching below the left temple, commencing with fine stinging, [3] . ► *Cold feeling on a small place at the left side of the head, a couple of inches above the ear, [2] . ► When bending the head to the left, pain as if a bundle of muscular fibres had been displaced in consequence of some violent exertion; the pain extends over the left temple, and behind the ear towards the left shoulder, increasing and decreasing synchronously with the pulse, [2] . ► Pressure in both sides of the head, from within outwards, [4] . ► Pressure in the left side of the occiput, which moves to the side of the head (after three minutes), [2] . ► He feels the pulsations of the arteries in the occiput, afterwards in the whole of the body, [4] . ► *Tension of the whole scalp, making the air feel painful , [4] . EYES. ► Warm feeling and slight pressure in the eyes; they have lost a good deal of their lustre, and look weaker, [4] . ► *Painful feeling of dryness in the interior of the eye , [2] . ► Feeling of dryness and a drawing in the eyes, [4] . [50.] ► Pressure in the left eye, [2] . ► As soon as he reads, sensation in each eye as if pressed asunder, [4] . ► Pulselike, tearing pain in the interior of the left eye (after one and a half hours), [4] . ► *Slight swelling of the upper left eyelid, cannot endure much reading, [4] . ► *Dry burning in the eyelids and the inner canthi, especially in the left eye, [2] . ► Twitching of the lower right eyelid, [3] . ► Cold feeling in the outer canthus of the right eye, as if caused by a cold breath, [2] . ► Sensation of twitching in the left upper eyelid, from within outwards, in paroxysms; it occurs only when holding the eyelid still; as soon as he raises the lid to look at something, the twitching disappears (after nine hours), [4] . ► Crawling under the upper lid, especially of the left eye, [3] . ► Obscuration of vision (after quarter of an hour), [3] . [60.] ► He does not perceive the objects which surround him, [3] . EARS. ► The whole external right ear is hot to the touch; this symptom frequently returns during the whole proving, [4] . ► Warm feeling in the region of the right external meatus auditorius, with sensation as if a thin membrane were stretched across it (after half an hour), [4] . ► Sensation in the left ear, both on the outer and inner side, as if the cartilages of the ear drew towards each other, [5] . ► Both ears feel stopped in front, [3] . ► Sensation in the outer meatus auditorius as if narrower, [5] . ► *The sensation of tension and pressure on the right meatus auditorius almost always remains the same, and afterwards extends to the left lower jaw, accompanied, when violent, with an increased secretion of apparently cold saliva from the right side (after half an hour), [4] . ► Continued pain owing to pressive tension in the region of the meatus auditorius , [4] . ► Sensation as if a membrane were stretched across the right meatus auditorius externus; tensive pressure in it, uninterruptedly, for seven days; worse during cold weather, [4] . ► He imagines a membrane is stretched across the meatus auditorius, with a sensation as if it were compressed (after quarter of an hour), [4] . [70.] ► He imagines a membrane is stretched across the right meatus auditorius (immediately), [4] . ► Pressure behind and below the left ear, [5] . ► Twinging sensation in both the external and internal ear, [15] . ► He hears worse with the right ear than with the left (after one hour), [4] . ► Dull roaring in the left ear, resembling the distant roaring of the wind; shrill singing in the right ear, [3] . ► Diminished hearing of the left ear, as if closed with the hand; it feels as if the walls of the meatus were nearer one another, or as if the ears were stopped with cotton, [3] . NOSE. ► Discharge of bloody mucus from the nose, [6] . ► Dry coryza; the left nostril stopped up, [2] . ► Tingling in the nose, as if caused by the copper pole of the galvanic battery, causing a sneezing after many unsuccessful attempts, and a discharge of a clear fluid, [2] . FACE. ► Warm feeling in the cheeks (after ten hours), [4] . [80.] ► Warm feeling in the left cheek (after four hours), [2] . ► Contractive pain of the left cheek, accompanied by gentle thrusts, as with a pointed instrument, and drawing pains in the third molar tooth, [3] . ► Fine stinging of the right cheek, [3] . ► Burning-stinging pain in the left cheek, [3] . ► Dryness of the inner side of the lower lip, [3] . ► Cutting cramp pain in the articulation of the lower jaw , [3] . MOUTH. ► Cold feeling in the upper incisors, as from a cool breath, [2] . ► Sensation in left row of teeth, as if hollow, [5] . ► Tongue coated white (after twenty-six hours), [4] . ► Burning sensation across the middle of the tongue; afterwards, burning and dryness in the whole mouth (after twenty minutes), [2] . [90.] ► Smarting sensation on the tongue and gums, [2] . ► Frequent contractive sensation in the mouth, producing an accumulation of watery saliva, [4] . ► Accumulation of much cool saliva in the mouth, [2] . ► The saliva in the mouth appears to be quite tenacious (after twenty-four hours), [4] . ► The saliva in the mouth was burning hot when discharging it (after half an hour), [2] . ► Sensation as if the breath and saliva were hot, without, however, the mouth feeling dry, [4] . ► Mucus in the mouth, with a sweetish, insipid taste, [3] . ► Taste in the mouth as of a foul stomach, [4] . ► Bread tastes bitter, [4] . ► Bread, eaten without butter, tastes bitter (in the evening), [3] . [100.] ► When smoking, tobacco tastes bitter, [3] . ► Smoking tobacco gives him no pleasure, [4] . THROAT. ► Tough mucus in the throat, which he is unable to hawk up, for eight days, [4] . ► *Dryness of the throat, with stinging, [3] . ► Inspiration irritates the throat and excites a cough, [4] . ► Scraping in the throat, [2] . ► Stitches in the throat, and constriction, on account of which the breathing is short and jerking; the constriction was relieved for a short time by the hacking cough, [4] . ► Difficult deglutition, as if the cervical glands were swollen, [4] . STOMACH. ► Hunger, early in the morning, [3] . ► Frequent, empty eructations, [2] . [110.] ► Imperfect eructations, reaching only the upper part of the chest, [3] . ► Rising of air from the stomach, when walking in the open air; as it issues, he has to yawn twice, after which he was troubled for a whole hour by eructations and abundant emission of flatulence, [2] . ► Hiccough (after one and a half hours), [4] , [5] . ► Nausea (one hour), [5] . ► Nausea and loathing, with shuddering (immediately), [2] . ► *Nausea and inclination to vomit , with pressure in the forehead, and a quantity of water accumulating in the mouth, [4] . ► Nausea in the stomach , with disinclination to attend to business; laziness, and sensation as if without a head, [4] . ► Nausea, inducing shivering, [4] . ► Continued nausea and inclination to vomit, in the fauces, [4] . ► Empty retching, water collecting in his mouth (after half an hour, and one and a half hours), [4] . [120.] ► The retching increases in violence, in proportion as it becomes more frequent; the eyes become full of water, [4] . ► During the retching, all the symptoms increase, only the stupid feeling in the head decreases , [4] . ► Vomiting, with great anguish, [7] . ► Vomiting (one hour after the first attack of retching), with great exertion of the stomach, in five or six paroxysms, every paroxysm being accompanied with a sensation as if the head, in the region of the ears, would burst; only a small quantity of greenish, sourish juice is thrown up from the stomach (after one and a half hours), [4] . ► Vomiting, with great exertion, and violent pressure on the stomach; the effort to vomit takes away his breath, and almost suffocates him, nevertheless, nothing but a quantity of sourish water is thrown up (after two and a half hours), [4] . ► Vomiting, with exertion of the stomach, and violent compression in the epigastrium, and a similar sensation in the head (after two and a half hours), [4] . ► (Vomiting is followed by a diminution of the pains in the head), [4] . ► Vomiting, diarrha, death (in a strong man), [3] . ► Fulness in the stomach, with hunger, [3] . ► Pinching in the stomach (after one and a half hours), [5] . [130.] ► Slight pinching in the stomach, or just above it, [3] . ► Pressure in the stomach, as with a dull point, [5] . ► Pressure in the region of the stomach, during an inspiration, [4] . ► Hard pressure on the region and pit of the stomach, two days in succession, [4] . ► Troublesome pressure on the pit of the stomach, which makes him unable to decide whether he is hungry or not, the whole day, [4] . ► Cutting in the epigastric region (after two hours), [4] . ► Occasional sharp cutting on different parts of the epigastric region, abating after the emission of flatulence, [4] . ABDOMEN. ► Constrictive sensation in the region of the diaphragm, [4] . ► Breaking, in the abdomen, of the flatus not emitted, [3] . ► Painless and silent movement of flatulence in the abdomen, [2] . [140.] ► Qualmishness in the abdomen, with repeated attacks of oppressive headache along the coronal suture (after two hours), [2] . ► Fulness in abdomen, with appetite and hunger, [3] . ► Pressure in the abdomen, [4] . ► Excessive colic and vomiting, [9] . ► Cutting in the abdomen, and sharp stitches in the rectum from above downwards, previous to stool (early in the morning), [4] . ► Sensation of pressure and painful bearing down on the left side of the abdomen, felt during motion, [4] . ► Single, painful sensations in the left side of the abdomen, in an oblique direction below the umbilicus, [2] . ► Raging, intense pain in the left groin, darting through the urethra into the glans, and causing a violent, sore, smarting, contractive pain in the same, for a long time, [2] . STOOL AND ANUS. ► Diarrha, [9] , [11] . ► Diarrha, the stools resembling resin, and consisting of tough mucus; for six days, he passes shaggy masses of mucus, with ascarides, [2] . [150.] ► *One hour and a half after stool, he has another pressing desire for stool, with cutting in the abdomen and rectum, before and during stool , which is softer than the former, [4] . ► *The usual morning stool delayed for a couple of hours; it was scanty, yellow (mucous), and came out in a thin string, [2] . ► Stool, consisting of small, hard pieces, [4] . ► Stool whitish-gray and ash-colored, the first part like bloody mucus, [1] . URINARY ORGANS. ► Pressure upon the bladder, during and after the emission of urine, [3] . ► (Drawing in the urethra), [1] . ► Constant desire to urinate, [3] . SEXUAL ORGANS. ► Miscarriage (abortion), [10] . RESPIRATORY APPARATUS. ► Several attacks of cough, brought on by mucus in the chest, which rises into the throat, and causes difficult breathing, and, lastly, cough with expectoration, [3] . ► Much expectoration by hawking and coughing, [1] . [160.] ► (Whistling respiration when beginning to cough), [1] . ► Very short breath (at night), [1] . ► Short breathing; the throat feels constricted, and he is attacked with a hacking cough, [4] . CHEST. ► Visible twitchings and jactitations of the muscles in the region of the clavicle, [5] . ► Pain around both lungs, as if constricted with a sharp wire, [4] . ► Feeling of pressure in the whole chest, [4] . ► Sharp pressure in the region of the last ribs, as with the back of a knife, [4] . ► Stitches in the chest when taking an inspiration (after twenty-four hours), [4] . ► Dull stitches in both lungs when taking a rather deep inspiration, [4] . ► Frequent , dull stitches in both lungs, during an inspiration , for eight days, [4] . [170.] ► Burning sensation in the right half of the chest, more external than internal, [5] . ► Painful stretchings in the left side (after three-quarters of an hour), [3] . ► Constrictive sensation in the left lobe of the lungs, as if a wire or string were twisted round, cutting it through, [4] . ► Strong pressing in the right side of the chest, at regular intervals (after one and a half hours), [5] . ► Stitches in the right lobe of the lungs during an inspiration (after twelve hours), [4] . ► Dull stitch on the left side near the pit of the stomach (after nine hours), [4] . ► Dull stitch, arresting the breathing, deep, apparently in the left lung, at every inspiration (after fifteen hours), [4] . NECK AND BACK. ► Sensation in the muscles of the nape of the neck as if a cravat were tied too tight, as if the parts were pressed upon by a blunt edge, [3] . ► Spasmodic contractions of the left cervical muscles, accompanied by a perceptible bending of the head towards the left side, [2] . ► Feeling of weight about the neck, and sensation as if the muscles were compressed by a cravat, [5] . [180.] ► Pain in the left side of the nape of the neck, as if a bundle of muscular fibres had been displaced by violent exertion; the pain afterwards extends over the head and shoulders (after six hours), [2] . ► *Paralytic pain in one of the muscles of the nape of the neck, as if the parts were bruised moving them, [4] . ► Dull stitches below the scapulæ, [4] . ► *Pain, as from a contusion or a bruise, along the inner border of the right scapula, especially when touching the scapula, or moving it inwards (after twenty-five hours), [2] . ► *Bruised pain in the back, [4] . ► Paralytic pain in the back, as if bruised; felt standing or sitting; disappearing when lying down, [4] . ► Burning pain, with stitches, in the small of the back, while sitting, [3] . ► Pain across the spinal column, from one brim of the pelvis to the other, as if the flesh were torn and pulled toward the outer side, in tearing jerks, when walking, [5] . EXTREMITIES IN GENERAL. ► *Lightness of all the limbs ; he is not aware of having a body, [3] . ► Occasional darting and tearing pains in the upper and lower limbs, [4] . [190.] ► Bruised feeling, and sometimes a transient, painful tearing in the upper and lower limbs, [4] . UPPER EXTREMITIES. ► Paralytic weakness in the arm, [4] . ► He is unable to let his arm lie upon the table without feeling exhausted; he feels no pain when the arm hangs down, [4] . ► Tearing ache in the left arm, in every position, [4] . ► Contractive, tensive pain of the deltoid muscle, when laying the hand upon the table, or when it lies there, [3] . ► Pain, as from a sprain in the shoulder, when moving the arm, [3] . ► Violently tearing stitches in both shoulders, both when in motion and at rest, [4] . ► Pressure in the left axilla, as with a rough piece of wood, [5] . ► Sudden, dull pain in the axilla, apparently in the axillary glands, [2] . ► Itching, as if caused by a flea-bite, at a place below the right axilla, in front, [3] . [200.] ► Drawing, paralytic pain in the left wrist-joint, [2] . ► Sudden drawing and burning pain, extending from the wrist-joint through the thumb and index finger (after three hours), [5] . ► Drawing in the fingers, evenings, when lying in bed, [1] . LOWER EXTREMITIES. ► Feeling of lassitude in the lower limbs, as if not rested sufficiently by sleep, [4] . ► Lassitude of the lower limbs, when going upstairs, for many days, [4] . ► Weariness and bruised feeling of the lower limbs and knees, as in the beginning of an intermittent fever, [4] . ► Painful feeling in the hip, [3] . ► When stepping, violent pain in the hip-joint, and in the middle of the thigh; the foot feels paralyzed from it; he cannot step on the foot well, [2] . ► Drawing ache in the hips (when walking), [5] . ► Dull pressure in the right hip , [5] . [210.] ► Dull pain in the hip-joint and in the middle of the thigh, when touching the parts, or when walking, or when moving about after being seated, [2] . ► Sensation from the right hip-joint to the knee, as if the limb would go to sleep, [2] . ► Drawing and tensive pain in the head of the left femur, and further on in the bone, especially when walking, [2] . ► Drawing in the hamstrings, evenings, when lying in bed, [1] . ► Sudden grinding pain in the upper muscles of the left thigh, [3] . ► Lancinating, tearing pain in the left thigh, [4] . ► Spasmodic contractions of the muscles of the right thigh, near the knees, abating when extending the limb, [3] . ► Feeling of lassitude in the knees, with visible staggering when walking, it not very careful (after fifteen minutes), [2] . ► Drawing in the knee, [1] . ► Pressure above the bend of the right knee, as from something hard or blunt, [5] . [220.] ► Uneasiness in the left knee-joint, inducing one to move about (after half an hour), [3] . ► Violent tearing stitching in the knees, during motion and at rest, [4] . ► (Clucking in the patella), [1] . ► The left leg feels as if gone to sleep, and the foot is insensible as when very cold; it looks dead, [3] . ► Feeling as from bruises in the left tibia, [5] . ► Visible twitches and jactitations in the calf muscles, [5] . ► Sudden stitching pain in the sole of the foot (after three and a half hours), [5] . ► Drawings in the toes, evenings, in bed, [1] . ► Both little toes are painful, as if frozen, [2] . GENERALITIES. ►► *Excessive sensibility of all the nerves, when merely imagining (which he is constantly obliged to do), that some one is scratching even slightly on linen, or some similar substance, with the finger-tips or nails; a most disagreeable sensation thrills through him, momentarily arresting all his thoughts and functions (after eleven hours), [4] . [230.] ► *Laziness, slowness, and indisposition to work, [4] . ► Great lassitude after dinner, [2] . ► *Great faintness and constant yawning, every afternoon, [4] . ► General, weary feeling, sometimes as if bruised all over, [4] . ► Towards evening, he becomes so faint and nauseated, that he feels as if he would die, as soon as he rises from his seat; he is obliged to go to bed, [4] . ► Ebullition of blood, evenings, in bed, for two hours, prevents sleeping, [1] . ► General uneasy feeling and nausea, [4] . SLEEP AND DREAMS. ► Frequent yawning, [4] . ► Great drowsiness by day (after twelve, thirteen, and fourteen days), [4] . ► Drowsy; out of humor, [2] . ► Restless sleep (every other night cannot sleep well), [1] . [240.] ► During sleep, such violent stitches in the dorsum of the left foot, that he dreamed he experienced a stitch, while a blister of Cantharides was applied to his foot; he felt nothing on waking up, [5] . ► Nightly vexing dreams about insults received, [3] . FEVER. ► Shuddering over the whole body (immediately). ► Slight shuddering over the body (after a half and one and a half hours), [4] . ► Shuddering (with disgust and nausea), (immediately), [3] . ► Chilliness and shivering, without any thirst, [4] . ► Uninterrupted chilliness; goose-skin; hands and face cold; blueness of the face, [4] . ► Slight chills in the back (suddenly brought on by biting upon a hard crust), [2] . ► Chilliness when drinking, [4] . ► Chilliness the whole day; when sitting or lying still, and keeping covered, he feels nothing, except a soreness of the eyes, a pressure on the forehead and on the pit of the stomach, and sometimes external heat, but when taking ever so little exercise in the room, or when exposed to the open air, without taking exercise, he feels an excessive chilliness, with scarcely any thirst; when walking fast in the open air, or entering a warm room after coming out of the open air, or when becoming heated by violent talking in the room, or after dinner, or when lying in a warm bed, he feels healthy, naturally warm, even a little hot, and has a desire for beer, [4] . [250.] ► Shaking chills in the evening, with extreme faintness, especially in the knees and the small of the back, without any thirst; hands cold, rest of body naturally warm, forehead hot, [4] . ► Chills, with heat in the face, [4] . ► Chilliness when not well covered, or when moving; heat as soon as he covers himself, sometimes with chills, [4] . ► Cold feeling over the body, as if a cold wind blew upon him; at the same time ha was cold to the touch, with goose-skin, the warmth returning in a few hours, and being somewhat increased (in the afternoon), with a slimy mouth, dryness in the throat, and thirst; afterwards another attack of coldness as before; and in the evening, an hour previous to going to sleep, an increase of warmth, continuing while in bed: he is obliged to uncover his hands, with great dryness of the palate, [3] . ► Fever the whole day; chilliness in the afternoon, abating neither during exercise in the open air nor by external warmth; hot feeling externally, with internal chills and thirst, after dinner, [4] . ► Chilliness after the hot feeling, the heat of the head and face remaining; chilly at the slightest motion, [4] . ► Hands icy cold, arms and rest of body warm; still they are covered with goose-skin, and he has violent chills, [4] . ► Heat of the forehead and hairy scalp, the rest of the body being naturally warm, with slight chills and chilliness; no thirst; pulse strong and quick, [4] . ► Hot feeling and actual heat after the chills, especially in the face and palm of the hand, the symptoms in the ear appearing again, [4] . ► Unusual warmth of the body, the whole day (after twenty-four hours), [3] . [260.] ► Warm feeling, as if sweat would break out (after four hours), [4] . ► He sweats easily, even from a slight cause, [4] . ► Warm sweat, even when sitting still, [4] . ► Slight sweat only, on the upper part of the body and on the upper limbs, [5] . ► Sweat, evenings, in bed, immediately after lying down, [1] . ► Profuse night-sweat, [4] . CONDITIONS. ► Aggravation. ► ( Morning ), Early, when rising, dizziness in head, etc.; early, when rising, throbbing pain in the forehead; early, hunger; previous to stool, cutting in abdomen, etc. ► ( Noon ), Drawing headache. ► ( Afternoon ), Great faintness, etc.; warmth increased; chilliness. ► ( Towards evening ), Becomes faint, etc. ► ( Evening ), Vertigo; bread tastes bitter; when lying in bed, drawing in fingers; when lying in bed, drawing in hamstrings; in bed drawings in toes; ebullition of blood; shaking chills; an hour before going to bed, increase of warmth; in bed, immediately after lying down, sweat. ► ( Night ), Very short breath; profuse sweat. ► ( Every other night ), Cannot sleep well. ► ( When exposed to open air, without taking exercise ), Excessive chilliness. ► ( Walking in open air ), Rising of air from stomach, etc. ► ( During cold weather ), Sensation of a membrane across auditory passage. ► ( Before cough ), Angry, etc. ► ( When not well covered ), Chilliness. ► ( After dinner ), Lassitude; hot feeling externally, etc. ► ( When drinking ), Chilliness. ► ( After getting dry again ), Vertigo, headache, burning on tongue and in the mouth, contraction of left cervical muscles, and weakness in knees, return again. ► ( During an inspiration ), Pressure on region of stomach; stitches in chest; stitches in both lungs; dull stitches in lungs; stitching in right lobe of lungs ; stitch, apparently in left lung. ► ( When attempting to perform intellectual labor ), Thoughts vanish, etc. ► ( Laying hand on table ), Pain in deltoid muscle. ► ( Letting arm lie on table ), Feels exhausted. ► ( When hand lies on table ), Pain in deltoid muscle. ► ( Motion ), Pressure, etc., on side of abdomen; chilliness. ► ( Moving the part inward ), Pain along border of scapula. ► ( When moving arm ), Pain in shoulder. ► ( When moving about after being seated ), Dull pain in hip-joint, etc. ► ( Attempting to reflect ), Pains in head, etc. ► ( Retching ), Pressure in brain; all symptoms, except stupid feeling in head . ► ( Rising from seat and walking about ), Vertigo. ► ( Taking exercise in room ), Excessive chilliness. ► ( Shaking head ), Headache in temple. ► ( Sitting ), Pain in back; pain, etc.; in small of back. ► ( Standing ), Pain in back. ► ( Holding eyelid still ), Sense of twitching in upper eyelid. ► ( Stepping ), Pain in hip-joint, etc. ► ( During sleep ), Stitches in dorsum of foot. ► ( Before stool ), Cutting in rectum. ► ( During stool ), Cutting in rectum. ► ( Stooping ), Throbbing pain in forehead. ► ( After stooping and raising head again ), Tearing in forehead. ► ( Touching the parts ), Pain along border of scapula; dull pain in hip-joint, etc. ► ( During and after emission of urine ), Pressure upon the bladder. ► ( Walking ), Pain in left temple, etc.; pain across spinal column; ache in hips; pain in hip-joint; pain in head of left femur, etc. ► ( Walking upstairs ), Lassitude of lower limbs. ► Amelioration. ► ( Open air ), Drawing headache. ► ( Walking in open air ), Headache disappeared, together with hot feeling in cheek, the drowsiness, and ill-humor. ► ( Bending head to left ), Pain as if muscular fibres displaced. ► ( Washing face with cold water ), Vertigo, headache, burning on tongue and in mouth, contraction of left cervical muscles, and weakness in the knees disappeared. ► ( Hacking cough ), Constriction in throat. ► ( Extending the limb ), Contraction of thigh-muscles. ► ( Emission of flatulence ), Ache in epigastric region. ► ( When lying down ), Headache; paralytic pain

Vertigo, as from slight intoxication, on rising from a seat and walking about (aft. 10 m.). He does not notice things about him. Mental condition as if just falling asleep; a gradual vanishing of the thoughts. Thoughts so overstrained that they vanish completely. He is quite stupid in his head and has no inclination for anything. [L. Rkt.]. Incapacity for any work, and he can do nothing; his mental powers fail him (before each attack of vomiting, afterwards somewhat better); as a rule his reason is defective all throughout the medicinal disease. Sensation of vertigo, as if he could not stand very surely, in the evening (aft. 4 d.). Confusion, like stupidity of the whole head, with tension in the region of the ears. [f:-h1]. In the morning, on rising, dizzy in the head, with headache in the left side of the forehead (aft. 22 h.). When he wishes to work with his head and to reflect, the want of thinking power immediately returns and the drawing pressure in the forehead, so that he must immediately leave off. As often as he attempts to reflect a little, the head affections and the sick feeling increase perceptibly; he must quickly cease thinking, which would besides be in vain, as he is quite stupid. Aching, stupefying, dull headache in the forehead, as if he had been wakened too soon from sleep. Dull headache (aft. 1/2 h.). Headache, like confusion in the left temple, thereafter under the parietal bones, lastly in the occiput. Confusion of the head, less observable when walking, more when sitting, and pressing in the eyes as with a blunt point from within outwards, especially under the right eyelid (aft. 1/4 h.). Tensive painful confusion of the head. The head is heavy and confused, at the same time pressure above the sagittal suture, as if he were intoxicated (aft. 3 h.). The head becomes heavy, as if there were something that shook or swayed in it, which, after bending it forwards or backwards, lets its weight be felt. Pressure in the brain, chiefly anteriorly (aft. 3/4 h.). (f-h1]. Aching in the left side of the occiput extending towards the side of the head (aft. 3 m.). Out-pressing pain on both sides of the head. Very acute headache in the left temple and behind the ears, like compression, which becomes worse when walking and shaking the head, but is alleviated by sitting (aft. 12 h.). Pressure from without inwards over the greatest portion of the brain (aft. 2 3/4 h.). Pressure in the brain from above downwards as with a stone, on a spot of the forehead (aft. 1/4 h.). [f:h1]. Violent pressure downwards in the forehead upon the eyes, which then water (aft. 2 1/4h.). Pressure combined with other sensations here and there in the brain. [L. Rkt.]. Sensation of pressure of alternating severity from above downwards in the forehead. Aching pain in the temples, especially the left. Sharp aching headache above the root of the nose. Tearing aching pain in the left temple. Violent, drawing pressure in the brain under the forehead (increased every time he retches). Drawing headache, as if it would draw into the temple (at noon); it appears to be alleviated in the open air and by lying. A (stupefying) drawing here and there in the brain, ear, and nape. When he has stooped and rises up again, for some seconds, lacerating pain in the forehead. Tearing, pulse-like throbbing pain in the forehead. (f-h1]. In the morning on rising from bed, throbbing pain in the forehead (aft. 24h.). By stooping forwards, throbbing headache is excited in the forehead. [L. Rkt.]. He feels the beat of the arteries in the occiput, afterwards throughout the body. [L. Rkt.]. Tension of the whole scalp so that he is (painfully) conscious of the hairs. Itching commencing with fine pricks under the left temple. A cold sensation on a small spot of the left side of the head, a couple of inches above the ear. Formication under the upper eyelid, especially of the left eye. The left upper eyelid is somewhat swollen; the eye cannot bear much reading. [L. Rkt.]. Twitching of the lower lid of the right eye. Sensation of twitching in the left upper eyelid from without outwards, by fits, but only when he holds the eyelid still; but when he elevates it in order to look at anything, it goes off immediately (aft. 9 h.). (f-h1]. In the outer canthus of the right eye a feeling of cold as from a cold breath. When he uses the eyes for reading, there occurs in each of them a feeling as if it was forced asunder. Pressure in the left eye. Tearing pain in the interior of the right eye synchronous with the pulse (aft. 1 1/2 h.). Sensation of dryness and drawing in the eyes. Dry burning in the eyelids and inner canthi, especially in the left eye. (Stf. ]. Painful dry feeling in the interior of the eye. Warm feeling and slight pressure in the eyes; they have lost much of their brightness and look duller. Obscuration of the eyes (aft. 1/4 h.). The whole of the right outer ear is hot to the touch, frequently recurring during the whole medicinal disease. Sensation of warmth at the orifice of the right meatus auditorius and feeling as if a thin skin were over it (aft. 1/2 h.). Dull roaring in the left ear, like a distant wind; in the right clear singing. In the ear, externally as well as internally, a pinching feeling, like earache. Aching behind and below the left ear. On bending the head towards the left, a pain, as if from over-exertion a fasciculus of muscular fibres was out of its place, which spreads over the left temple and behind the ear towards the left shoulder, and in unison with the pulse increases as that rises, and diminishes as it falls. Continued pressive and tensive pain at the orifice of the meatus auditorius. In the left ear a sensation observed outwardly and inwardly, as if the cartilages of the ear contracted. Diminished hearing of the left ear, as if it were closed by the hand; it is as if the cartilages went closer together, or as if cotton-wool plugged the ears. He feels as if a skin were stretched over the right meatus auditorius (immediately). A sensation in the external meatus as if the orifice in front of the membrana tympani were pasted up. In front of both ears he feels as if they were stopped. He hears worse with the right than with the left ear (aft. 1 h.). He feels as if a membrane were before the orifice of the meatus auditorius, with sensation as if it was compressed (aft. 1/4 h.). It is as if a membrane were stretched over the right meatus auditorius, with a tensive pressure therein-almost continuously for seven days, but always worse during the chill. The feeling of tension and pressure on the right meatus auditorius scarcely ever remits, and when it does it extends to the right lower jaw, and when it is severe a large quantity of apparently cold saliva flows from the right side of the mouth (aft. 1/2 h.). Burning, shooting pain on the left cheek. Feeling of warmth in the left cheek (aft. 4h.). Feeling of warmth in the cheeks (aft. 10 h.). On washing the face with cold water the vertigo, headache, burning on the tongue and in the mouth, contraction of the left cervical muscles and the weakness of the knees went off, but after drying the face they returned. [Stf. ]. Fine shooting on the right cheek. On the left cheek a contractive pain accompanied by gentle, but sharp blows, with drawing pain in the third molar tooth. Produces in the nose a discharge of bloody mucus. [Murray, Appar. Med., iti, p.519.]. Dryness of the inside of the lower lip. A feeling of coldness, like a cold breath, in the upper incisors. Sensation in the left row of teeth as if they were hollow. (f-h1]. Cutting pain, with cramp, at the maxillary joint. Much cool saliva collects in the mouth. Frequent sensation of contraction in the interior of the mouth, causing flow of watery saliva. The saliva in the mouth seems to be quite viscid (aft.24 h.). The saliva ejected was burning hot in the mouth (aft.1/2 h.). Tongue furred white (aft. 26 h.). A smarting sensation on the tongue and gums. Sensation of burning transversely across the middle of the tongue, then burning and dryness in the whole of the mouth (aft. 20 m.). (f-h1]. Mucus in the mouth, with sweetish flat taste. Taste in the mouth as if the stomach were deranged. Tobacco smoking is not relished. Tobacco when smoked tastes bitter. Bread tastes bitter. The dry bread eaten tastes bitter (in the evening). Dryness of the throat, with shooting. Scraping in the throat. In the throat such viscid mucus that he could not bring it up nor hawk it out, for eight days. Swallowing is difficult, as from swelling of the cervical glands. [L. Rkt.]. Hiccup (aft. 1 1/2 h.). Hunger in the morning. Frequent eructation. Frequent empty eructation. When walking in the open air a rising up as of air, out of the stomach, and when it came to the mouth he must yawn a couple of times, then for an hour empty eructation and copious discharge of flatus. Incomplete eructation up to the upper part of the chest. Shivering from nausea. Nausea (aft. 1 h.). General discomfort and nausea. Nausea and loathing with shuddering (immediately). Constant nausea and sickness in the fauces. Nausea, with aching in the forehead, and great flow of water into the mouth. Empty retching, with flow of water into the mouth (aft. 1/2, 1 1/2 h.). The retching is always more violent the oftener it comes; the eyes become filled with water. When retching all the symptoms are increased, except the stupidity of the head, which decreases. Vomiting (an hour after the first retching), with great straining of the stomach, in five or six paroxysms, each time as if the head would burst in the region of the ears; only a little greenish, slightly sour, gastric juice is ejected (aft. 1 1/2 h.). Vomiting, with great anxiety. [J. B. v. Helmont, Pharmac. med., Parag.47.] [fh1l]. Vomiting, diarrhea-death. [G. W. Wedel, Amoenit. Mat. Med., p.240.]. Vomiting with great effort and violent pressure in the stomach; the effort to vomit takes away his breath, so as almost to suffocate him, and yet nothing but sourish water is ejected (aft. 2 1/4 h.). Vomiting, with sensation of straining of the stomach and violent compression in the epigastrium, and a similar feeling in the head (aft. 2 1/4 h.). (L. Rkt.]. There always remains some nausea in the stomach, with disinclination to work, sensation of want of thinking power and laziness. (f-h1]. (After the vomiting alleviation of the head symptoms.) (f-h1]. Fulness in the stomach, with hunger. Pinching in the stomach (aft. 1 1/2 h.). Slight pinching in the stomach or just over it. In the stomach a pressure as with a blunt point. Troublesome pressure on the scrobiculus cordis, which prevents him feeling whether he is hungry or not-all day. Hard pressure on the region of the stomach and scrobiculus cordis for two successive days. Pressure on the gastric region on inspiring. Feeling of constriction in the region of the diaphragm. From time to time sharp cutting round about in the epigastrium, which is always relieved by the discharge of some flatulence. Cutting in the epigastrium (aft. 2 h.). Horrible colic and vomiting. [Coste and Willemet, in Samml. br. Abh. f. pr., ??? iv, 2.]. Fulness in the abdomen, and yet at the same time appetite and hunger. Qualmishness in the abdomen, with repeated aching pain along the coronal suture (aft. 8 h.). A painless and gentle working about in the abdomen. Explosion of flatulence in the abdomen which is not discharged. Pressure in the abdomen. Feeling of aching and painful pressing on the left side of the abdomen, observed when moving. Single painful sensations in the left side of the abdomen, obliquely below the navel. Before the stool cutting in the abdomen and sharp stitches in the rectum from above downwards (in the morning). He has hurried urging to stool (1 1/2 hour after the first stool) with cutting in the abdomen and rectum before and during the (looser) stool. [L. Rkt.]. Stool in hard small pieces. The usual morning stool was delayed for some hours, and then it was scanty, yellow like an egg (slimy) and of slender form. Diarrhea. [Coste and Willemet, l.c.]. Diarrheic, viscid slimy, as it were resinous stool; ascarides pass in shaggy masses of mucus, for six days. Stool whitish-grey and ash coloured, with bloody mucus on the top. Pressure on the bladder, during and after urinating. Constant urging to urinate. (A drawing in the urethra.). A wild, acute pain in the left groin, which darted quickly through the urethra into the glans penis, and there a smarting, contractive, violent, internal pain remained for a long time. Premature birth, expulsion of the fetus. [Ray, Hist. univ. Plant., i.]. (Stuffed coryza; the left nostril is stopped up). (Violent sneezing.). A tickling runs through the nose (as from the positive galvanic pole), which, after ineffectual urging, causes sneezing and a flow of clear fluid. (Stf. ]. Sensation as if the breath and saliva were hot, but without dry feeling in the mouth. Repeated coughing on account of mucus in the chest, which first rises up into the throat and causes difficulty of breath, and at length cough, with expectoration. Inspiration causes an irritation in the throat, which excites cough. [L. Rkt.]. Very short breath (at night). (Angry and cross before the cough.). Great expectoration of mucus by hawking and coughing. (At the commencement of the cough the respiration is whistling.). Short breath; the throat is constricted and this causes hacking cough. Obtuse stitch, impending respiration, quite low down, as if in the left lung, at every inspiration (aft. 15 h.). He can only breathe short and by jerks on account of stitches and constriction in the larynx; short coughing relieved the constriction for a little time. Obtuse stitch on the left near the scrobiculus cordis (aft. 9 h.). In the right lobe of the lung stitches when inspiring (aft. 12 h.). [L. Rkt.]. Frequent, obtuse stitches in both lungs, during inspiration, for eight days. Stitches in the chest during respiration (aft. 24 h.). When he takes a rather deep breath immediately obtuse stitches in both lungs. [L. Rkt.]. Sensation of pressure on the whole of the chest. In the region of the last ribs a sharp pressure as if with the back of a knife. In the right side of the chest a severe, forcible pressing at regular intervals (aft. 1 1/2 h.). Visible twitching and palpitation in the muscles of the region of the clavicle. Stretching pain in the left side (aft. 3/4 h.). Sensation as if the left lung were constricted or cut into by a string or wire. Pain round about in both lungs, as if they were constricted by a sharp wire. In the right side of the chest a burning sensation, more externally than internally. Burning pain with shooting in the sacrum, while sitting. From one crista ilii to the other over the spine, pain as if the flesh of the muscles were rent outwards, in tearing jerks, when walking. Paralytic pain, as if bruised, in the back, as long as he remains erect, stands or sits, but does not lie. Bruised pain in the back. [f:h1]. Obtuse stitches under the scapulae. At the inner border of the right scapula a pain as from a knock or blow, especially felt on touching the scapula or drawing it inwards (aft. 25 h.). (Stf. ]. Pain in the nape on the left side as if by over-exertion a bundle of muscular fibres had got out of their place, the pain then spreads over the head and shoulders (aft. 6 h.). (Stf. ]. Paralytic pain in one of the muscles of the nape, as if it were bruised, on moving the neck. On the muscles of the nape a sensation as from a too tight neck cloth, and as if a blunt knife were pressed on it. Feeling of weight on the neck and sensation as if the muscles were compressed by bandages. Spasmodic contraction of the left cervical muscles, with perceptible bending of the head to that side. Violent tearing stitches in both shoulders during motion and rest. [L. Rkt.]. In the axilla, as if in the axillary glands, a rapidly occurring obtuse pain. A pressure in the left axilla, as with a rough piece of wood. Under the right axilla towards the front an itching, as from a fleabite. Pain in the shoulder, as if dislocated, on moving the arm. On the deltoid muscle of the upper arm a contractive tensive pain, on laying the hand on the table, and also when it is left lying there. Paralytic weakness in the arm. He cannot keep the arm lying on the table long without discomfort and feeling of exhaustion; but when he allows the arm to hang down he feels nothing. Aching tearing in the left arm in all positions. Drawing paralytic pain in the left wrist-joint. Quick, drawing burning pain from the wrist through the thumb and index finger (aft. 3 h.). A drawing in the fingers, in the evening, while lying in bed. Occasional twitching tearing pains in the upper and lower extremities. Bruised feeling and sometimes transient painful tearing in the upper and lower extremities. Painful sensation on the hip. In the right hip an obtuse aching. In the head of the left thigh and beyond, especially when walking, a (drawing tensive) pain. In the hips a drawing aching pain (when walking). When walking or moving after sitting, as also when touching, a dull pain in the hip-joint and in the middle of the thigh. Sensation from the right hip-joint to the knee, as if the limb were going to sleep. [Stf. ]. When he treads he has a violent pain in the hip-joint and in the middle of the thigh, and the limb is as if paralyzed by it; he cannot tread properly. (Stf. ]. Sudden, digging pain in the upper muscles of the left thigh. Tearing, shooting pain in the left thigh. Spasmodic contraction of the muscles of the right thigh, near the knee, which goes off on stretching out the leg. A drawing in the knee. A drawing in the tendons of the hough, in the evening while lying in bed. [f.-h1]. (Clucking in the hough.). Violent tearing stitches in the knees, on moving and when at rest. [L. Rkt.]. Fatigue of the thighs on going upstairs, for many days. (f-h1]. Exhausted feeling in the thighs as if he had not obtained rest by sleep. Feeling of weakness in the knees with visible staggering when walking, when he does not take great care (aft. 15 m.). Exhaustion and bruised feeling of the thighs and knees, as in a fit of ague. Restlessness in the left knee-joint, which forces him to move (aft. 1/2 h.). Above the right hough a pressure as with some hard blunt substance. {Hbg.]. The left leg is as if asleep, and the foot, as when very cold, insensible and as if dead. Visible twitching and palpitation in the muscles of the calf. In the left tibia sensation as if bruised. In the sole of the foot quick shooting pains (aft. 3 1/2 h.). A drawing in the toes, in the evening when lying in bed. (On both feet the little toes are painful as if frozen.). Over sensitiveness of all the nerves; when he thinks (which he cannot help doing constantly) of someone scratching gently with the finger-tip or nail on linen or something similar, a very disagreeable sensation runs through him, which for an instant stops all his thoughts and actions (aft. 11 h.). After dinner great fatigue. Every afternoon great exhaustion and constant yawning. Laziness, slowness, and dislike to all work. General feeling of exhaustion and sometimes bruised feeling. Agility in all the limbs; he does not know that he has a body. When walking in the open air the headache, heat of the cheeks and sleepy crossness go off. [fth1]. When walking in the open air he seems to be floating like a disembodied spirit. Frequent yawning. Towards evening he becomes so weak, with sick feeling, that when he sits upright he feels as if he would every moment succumb and die, he must lie down in bed. [L. Rkt.]. Very great drowsiness by day (aft. 12, 13, 14 d.). (f-h1]. Drowsiness, crossness. (On alternate nights restless sleep; he cannot readily fall asleep.). During sleep such a violent shooting in the dorsum of the left foot, that he dreamt he got a stab during the application of a blister; on awaking he felt nothing. At night vexatious and annoying dreams of being put to shame, c. In the evening, in bed, an ebullition in the blood that prevented him going to sleep, for two hours. Shaking all over the body (immediately). Slight shiver over the body (aft. 1/2, 1 1/2 h.). Shiver (with loathing and nausea) (immediately). A rigor in the back (which came on suddenly on biting a hard crust). Rigor and chilliness, without thirst. Uninterrupted chilliness, goose-skin; hands and face cold, blueness of complexion. The hands are icy cold, but the arms and the rest of the body warm, yet covered with goose-skin, and he is very chilly. In the evening rigor with extreme weakness, especially in the knees and sacrum, without thirst; the hands are cold, but the rest of the body as warm as usual, the forehead, however, hot. Shivering with heat in the face. All day long chilliness; when he sits still or lies and keeps himself covered, he feels nothing (except a pain in the eyes, pressure in the forehead and on the scrobiculus cordis, and sometimes external heat); but when he moves about in the room ever so little, or sits out in the open air without moving, he is excessively chilly, almost entirely without thirst; but when he walks quickly out of doors or comes from the open air into the warm room, or when he becomes heated by animated conversation in the room, or after dinner, as also when lying in the warm bed, he feels well and warm enough, he has, indeed, some heat with thirst for beer. Chill when drinking. Cold sensation on the body, as if a cold wind blew on him; at the same time he felt cold to the touch, almost always with goose-skin; after a few hours warmth returned somewhat increased (in the afternoon), with slimy mouth, dryness in the throat and thirst; hereafter a similar coldness, and in the evening (an hour before bedtime) again increased warmth, which lasted in bed, during which he must put the hands outside the bed-clothes, also with great dryness on the palate. All day long fever : in the forenoon chilliness, which does not go off either by moving in the open air or by external warmth; after the midday siesta external feeling of heat, with internal rigor and thirst. When seated and not well covered up, or when he moves, immediately chilliness, but when he covers himself up he instantly becomes hot, though sometimes with rigor. After the cessation of the hot feeling, while heat of the head and face remains, chilliness comes on, so that he shivers on the slightest movement. Heat of the forehead and scalp, the rest of the body of normal temperature, with shivering and chilliness without thirst, and strong and quick pulse. After the chilliness feeling of heat and actual heat, especially of the face and palms, whereupon the pains in the ear return. Feeling of warmth, as if sweat would break out (aft. 4 h.). Unusual warmth of the body all day (aft. 24 h.). Slight sweat only on the upper part of the body and the upper extremities. In the evening in bed, immediately after lying down, perspiration. [fih1]. Profuse night sweat. Warm perspiration, even when sitting still. He perspires very readily from a slight cause. Melancholy peevishness. Lachrymose sadness and anxiety. Great gaiety (aft. 6 to 12 h.), alternating with calmness or even sadness for some moments. Aurum (Gold, the well-known metal.) From vol. iv, 2nd edit., 1825

keynotes.text: A Text-Book of Materia Medica. (Characteristic, Analytical, and Comparative.) By Pr. Allen Corson Cowperthwaite, M. D., PH. D., LL. D. ASARUM. (Asarum Europoeum.) Natural order.- Aristolochiaceae. Common name.- Hazel Wort. General Analysis.- Acts upon the cerebro-spinal nervous system, and affects especially the mucous membrane of the alimentary tract. Characteristic symptoms. Mind.- (Asarum) Gradual vanishing of thoughts, as when falling asleep. Head.- (Asarum) Very sensitive, compressive headache, especially in left temple and behind the ears. Tension of scalp (Caust.), making hair feel painful. Eyes.- (Asarum) Painful dryness of the interior of the eyes. Ears.- (Asarum) Pressure and tension in region of meatus auditorius. Mouth.- (Asarum) Disgusting taste in the mouth. Stomach.- (Asarum) Nausea and inclination to vomit. Frequent empty eructations. Want of appetite, even nauseated by food. Empty retching ; during the retchings all the symptoms are aggravated, except the stupid feeling about the head, which decreases. Violent cutting colic, with vomiting. Unquenchable longing for alcohol. (Carb. ac.) Stool.- (Asarum) Before stool, cutting in the abdomen, and sharp stitches in the rectum, from above downward. Limbs.- (Asarum) Lightness of all the limbs (Mez.) ; when walking seems to be gliding through the air ; dull pressure in the right hip. Generalities.- (Asarum) Excessive sensibility of all the nerves ( Cinch ., Coff. c.) ; when merely thinking (and this he must continually) that someone might, with the finger tip or nail, scratch even lightly on linen or similar material, a most disagreeable sensation thrills through him, arresting momentarily all his thoughts and actions. Therapeutic Range.- (Asarum) Mostly used in gastric and bilious disorders, where there is excessive nervous sensitiveness. Conditions.- (Asarum) In nervous, excitable temperament ; in drunkards. Compare.- (Asarum) Cupr., Nux v., Phos., Puls. Antidotes.- (Asarum) Camphor, Vinegar, vegetable acids. Copyright © Robert Séror 2003

indications.text: Preparations: Compound Syrup of Asarum - Compound Tincture of Lobelia Related entry: Asarum Europaeum.—Asarabacca
The rhizome and rootlets of the Asarum canadense, Linné. Nat. Ord.—Aristolochiaceae. COMMON NAMES: Wild ginger, Indian ginger, Canada snakeroot, and (improperly) Coltsfoot.

Botanical Source.—This American plant closely resembles the asarabacca of Europe (A. Europaeum). It has a creeping, fleshy, somewhat jointed, yellowish rhizome, with rootlets. From it ascends a quite short stem, which is forked near the surface of the soil, and each branch bears a reniform leaf, downy on both sides, 3 or 4 inches by 3 or 5, the petiole of which is long, round, and hairy. Upon a pendulous, hairy peduncle, growing from the fork of the stem, is a solitary flower, having a very wooly calyx, consisting of 3 broad, concave, pointed leaflets, of a brownish, dull-purple or greenish color on the inside, at top and bottom, depending on the amount of light which the plant enjoys, terminated by a long, spreading, inflected point, with reflect sides. The corolla is wanting. The fruit is a coriaceous, 6-celled capsule.

Description.—Wild ginger occurs in commerce in irregularly 4-angled pieces, about 9 to 5 inches in length, and about ⅛ inch in diameter. They are crooked, ash-brown, or purple-brown in color, break with a short fracture, are pulverable, and whitish internally. About every half inch exhibits nodes, to which thin rootlets are attached. The inner structure is pithy, surrounded by a broken zone of woody fibers, all enclosed in thick bark, which contains oil cells. The rootlets are traversed by a ligneous, central portion, covered with thick bark. It is fragrant, spicy, and slightly bitter.

History.—Wild ginger is a native of the United States, growing in woods and mountains, and in rich soil along roadsides, flowering in May and June. The whole herb has a fragrant odor, and a spicy, amarous taste. The root is the part used, and yields its active principles to alcohol, and partially to water by infusion. The oil is now used in the making of perfumes, and in various toilet waters.

Chemical Composition.—Asarum contains an acrid, bitter, reddish resin, a pale, spicy, aromatic, volatile oil, gum, chlorophyll, fat, starch, and various salts. It probably, also, contains an alkaloid, as Power obtained a body in very small quantities, which reacted with alkaloid-group reagents. The coloring matter (yellow), as Prof. Power has shown, appears to be the same as the asarin found by Graeger in asarabacca. The oil consists of asarene (C10H16), a neutral principle, asarin (C12H16O2), asarol (C10H18O), and ethers of asarol-probably the acetic and valerianic-and is thought also to contain methyl-eugenol, a substance hitherto not observed in nature.

Action, Medical Uses, and Dosage.—Asarum canadense is a spicy, stimulating agent, causing perspiration, promoting expectoration, and possessing carminative properties. It may be advantageously added to tinctures and compounds to improve their flavor, and render them more stimulating. It is used in colic and other painful affections of the stomach and bowels where no inflammation exists, and in chronic pulmonary affections. It has been successfully used by Dr. J. R. Black in dropsy, with albumen in the urine. The warm infusion is an excellent agent to promote copious sweating, and may be substituted for Virginia snakeroot as a diaphoretic and in debility. Harsh, dry skin, with checked perspiration, in low febrile and inflammatory affections, not of the gastro-intestinal tract, and sudden colds, have been successfully treated with the warm infusion, which is also a prompt emmenagogue when amenorrhoea is due to recent colds. Dose of the powder, ½ drachm; of the tincture, ½ fluid drachm to 2 fluid drachms. Of the infusion (℥ss to aqua Oj), freely; used also as an errhine.
King's American Dispensatory, 1898, was written by Harvey Wickes Felter, M.D., and John Uri Lloyd, Phr. M., Ph. D.
